# Security Policy

## Supported versions

Only the latest stable LoopCompass release receives security fixes. Earlier releases should be
updated before requesting support. The `main` branch may contain unreleased fixes, but it is not a
supported distribution.

## Reporting a vulnerability

Do not open a public issue for a suspected vulnerability. Use
[GitHub private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/adammmmmm/LoopCompass/security/advisories/new)
and include:

- The affected version, file, or workflow.
- Reproduction steps or a minimal proof of concept.
- The expected impact and any known preconditions.
- Suggested mitigations, if available.

Reports will be assessed privately. When a report is accepted, maintainers will coordinate the
fix and disclosure through a GitHub Security Advisory. Credit will be offered unless the reporter
prefers to remain anonymous.

## Scope

Security reports may cover the portable skill and templates, maintainer scripts, validation and
release tooling, published release artifacts, or GitHub Actions workflows. Model-provider behavior,
general support questions, and issues without a security impact belong in the normal issue tracker.
